title: Lasiorrhiza congesta

intro: Lasiorrhiza congesta, also known as Australian knotweed, is a plant species native to Australia that belongs to the family Aristolochiaceae. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful for treating various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections. The bioactive constituents of Lasiorrhiza congesta include aristolochic acids, which have been found to exhibit cytotoxic and anti-cancer properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Lasiorrhiza congesta is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion to treat various ailments, as well as being used in the production of tinctures and capsules.
